key_evidence: Illinois SB 3444 backing (Apr 10-17, 2026 coverage): OpenAI actively supports liability shield that would exempt AI firms from responsibility for mass-casualty events (>100 deaths, >$1B damage, including CBRN). Experts describe as 'markedly weak approach to corporate liability.' Direct ACC/AB3 and SYS/S4 regression signal — opposing public-liability posture vs Anthropic's SB 3261.

Musk v. OpenAI trial begins April 27, 2026 in Oakland: jury selection start is a material INT/I5 event. Allegations include fraud re: nonprofit conversion; Musk amended filing (Apr 7) seeks Altman removal and unwind of for-profit structure. Regardless of outcome, trial record itself weakens INT/I4 (values alignment).
Perplexity class-action (Apr 1, 2026) alleges OpenAI/others shared user chat data with Google/Meta without consent — adds B5 consent concern.
No new reparative action, transparency disclosure, or consent/safety structural change in 14-day window.
